name: Vía Verde de Cartagena, Molino Tío Jarapa, Acueducto de Perín – Ruta Circular – Cartagena
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cartagena, Spain, offers diverse terrain, from coastal trails and elevated viewpoints to dry forests and areas with volcanic features. The region is characterized by its rugged Sierra Minera, impressive coastal cliffs, and mountain ranges like Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso, and Roldán. Riders can explore a mix of fast double tracks, technical singletracks, and routes through natural parks such as Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas y Peña del Águila Regional Park. This varied landscape provides a range of mountain biking experiences for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Cartagena The most popular mountain bike trail is Vía Verde de Cartagena, Molino Tío Jarapa, Acueducto de Perín – Ruta Circular – Cartagena, a 25.0 miles (40.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 57 minutes to complete. This route follows the Vía Verde, offering a straightforward path suitable for various riders. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Atalayón, Casa de Boletes, Batería de Castillitos – Ruta Circular – Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán, a moderate 12.8 miles (20.5 km) path. This route explores the elevated terrain of Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso, and Roldán, passing by the historic Batería de Castillitos. Local mountain bikers also love the Sierra Gorda, Ermita del monte Calvario, Castillo de San Julián, Trincabotijas – Cartagena, a 12.6 miles (20.2 km) trail leading through the Sierra Gorda area, often completed in about 2 hours 3 minutes. Mountain biking around Cartagena is defined by coastal paths, historic mining landscapes, and elevated mountain ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy tracks to more technical singletrack sections. The routes in Cartagena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 550 reviews. More than 3000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cartagena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cartagena?
A: Cartagena offers a wide variety of mountain bike trails, with nearly 200 routes available for exploration. These trails cater to different skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cartagena?
A: The terrain around Cartagena is incredibly diverse. You'll find coastal trails with impressive cliffs, elevated viewpoints, and routes through dry forests. The region features rugged mining landscapes in the Sierra Minera, mountain ranges like Sierra de la Muela and Roldán, and natural parks such as Calblanque. Expect a mix of fast double tracks, flat singletracks, and rocky sections with technical singletrack elements.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly mountain bike trails in Cartagena?
A: Yes, Cartagena has several options suitable for beginners. There are over 40 easy mountain bike routes. A good starting point is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e921320316/via-verde-de-cartagena-molino-tio-jarapa-acueducto-de-perin-ruta-circular-cartagena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Cartagena, Molino Tío Jarapa, Acueducto de Perín – Ruta Circular – Cartagena&lt;/a&gt;, which follows a straightforward path and is generally suitable for various riders.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see while mountain biking in Cartagena?
A: Many trails offer stunning views and pass by significant landmarks. You can explore the unique mining landscapes of the Sierra Minera, coastal cliffs, and historic sites. Specific highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7211523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas and Peña del Águila Regional Park&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6581220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Blancas Summit&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2939367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roldán Peak&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937300378/atalayon-casa-de-boletes-bateria-de-castillitos-ruta-circular-sierra-de-la-muela-cabo-tinoso-y-roldan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalayón, Casa de Boletes, Batería de Castillitos – Ruta Circular – Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, passes by the historic Batería de Castillitos.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Cartagena?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Cartagena are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e921320316/via-verde-de-cartagena-molino-tio-jarapa-acueducto-de-perin-ruta-circular-cartagena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Cartagena, Molino Tío Jarapa, Acueducto de Perín – Ruta Circular – Cartagena&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937300378/atalayon-casa-de-boletes-bateria-de-castillitos-ruta-circular-sierra-de-la-muela-cabo-tinoso-y-roldan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalayón, Casa de Boletes, Batería de Castillitos – Ruta Circular – Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso y Roldán&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Cartagena?
A: The mountain bike trails around Cartagena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 550 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, from coastal paths to rugged mountain ranges, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels. The unique blend of natural beauty and historical mining sites also receives positive feedback.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Cartagena?
A: Cartagena generally offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year due to its Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and less intense heat than the summer months. Winters are typically mild, making it a viable option for year-round riding, though some trails might be affected by occasional rain.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that offer coastal views?
A: Absolutely. Cartagena's coastline is a significant feature of its mountain biking landscape. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19387298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calblanque Beach – Viewpoint of Portman Bay loop from Playa Honda&lt;/a&gt; showcase impressive coastal cliffs and often begin or end at scenic coves. The region's natural environment includes areas with volcanic features and historic islands, providing stunning sea views.

Q: Can I find trails that go through natural parks or protected areas?
A: Yes, several routes traverse protected natural areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7211523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calblanque, Monte de las Cenizas y Peña del Águila Regional Park&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent example, offering diverse habitats and forming a significant part of the biking landscape. These trails allow riders to experience the region's rich biodiversity.

Q: Are there any challenging or advanced mountain bike routes in Cartagena?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Cartagena offers over 20 difficult mountain bike routes. These often include technical singletrack sections, significant climbs, and rocky terrain. Areas within the Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso, and Roldán Natural Area provide elevated rides with demanding ascents and descents.

Q: What are some other points of interest near the mountain biking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore various attractions. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3794391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de San Francisco&lt;/a&gt; in Cartagena is a notable settlement highlight. For natural wonders, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/998557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolnuevo Sandstone Formations&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/620881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marchamalo Salt Flats&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4084641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Christ of the Divers&lt;/a&gt; is also a unique man-made monument worth seeing.
tips: The Tío Jarapa Mill is still in a fairly good state of preservation. It is one of the four windmills (the others are Zabala, Perín and Corona) that Juan Antonio Madrid Agüera built for each of his children. These windmills, which used sails instead of blades, were used primarily to grind flour and grain, grind salt, and extract water.
This aqueduct was built in 1945 to supply Cartagena with water.
Very hard section for newbies, I had to get off the bike and I ended up with my tongue hanging out even so
Kiosco Miguel is a good place to have a drink or a snack!

waypoints.name: Steep gravel climb in Parque Regional Sierra de la Muela, Cabo Tiñoso and Roldán
